txt: Browne, Simon, demy Magdalen Hall 1500, M.A. 7 Feb., 1508-9. See Bloxam, iv. 47.

Bodley, (Sir) Thomas, s. John, of Exeter; joined Magdalen Hall 1559; B.A. 26 July, 1563, (as Henry); fellow Merton Coll. 1563, M.A. 5 July, 1566, proctor 1569, founder of the Bodleian, born in Exeter 2 March, 1544, esquire, of the body to Queen Elizabeth 1583, (? M.P. Portsmouth 1584-5, and St. Germans 1586-7), resident at the Hague 1588-97, knighted at Whitehall 18 April, 1604, died in London 28 Jan., 1612, buried at the upper end of Merton College choir. See Ath., ii. 124; Gutch, i. 20; O.H.S., iv. 266; & D.N.B.
Roche, Dominic, of Ireland, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. untry under date circa 1564, aged 19; one of these names, Dominick Fitz Richard, M.P. Kinsale, 1613.
Firmiger, David, of Kent, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. in or before 1572, aged 18; B.A. 3 June, 1575.

Nic(h)olson, William, of Magdalen Hall in and before 1572, B.A. 15 April, 1573; fellow Corpus Christi Coll. 1573, M.A. 29 March, 1577; perhaps rector of Stapleford Tawney, Essex, 1572-96, and father of Thomas 1591. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Cæsar, (Sir) Julius, of Middlesex, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 16; B.A. 17 May, 1575, M.A. 18 Feb., 1577-8, D.C.L. 5 March, 1583-4 (LL.B. & LL.D. Paris 22 April, 1581), (J.C. alias Dalmarius or Adelmare, son of Cæsar Dalmarius or Adelmare, 'nobilis,' of Treviso in Italy), bencher of the Inner Temple 1590, treasurer 1593-4, judge of the Admiralty Court 1584, master in Chancery 1588, master extraordinary of Court of Requests 1591, knighted 20 May, 1603, chancellor and under-treasurer of the Exchequer 1606, P.C. 1607, master of the Rolls 1614-39, M.P. Reigate 1588-9, Blechingley 1593, New Windsor 1597-8 and 1601, Westminster circa 1607-11, Middlesex 1614, Maldon 1621-2, died 18 April, 1636, aged 79, buried at Great St. Helen's, Bishopsgate. See O.H.S., x. 378; Fasti, i. 224; D.N.B.; & Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Wignall, Richard, of London, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 19, B.A. 28 Nov., 1575, fellow All Souls' Coll. 1577, M.A. 22 June, 1579; incorporated at Cambridge 1584; vicar of Barking, Essex, 1592; licenced 4 July, 1592, to marry Mary, daughter of Thomas Barfote, of Hitchin, Herts. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Hughes, Robert, of Brasenose Coll. 1571, from Little Hereford, co. Hereford; B.A. from Magdalen Hall 12 July, 1578, where one of these names matriculated in 1574 from Bucks, gent., aged 17; mathematician and geographer; died at Kidlington, Oxon, 24 May, 1632, aged 79, buried in Christ Church. See Ath. ii. 534; Gutch, i. 503; Fasti, i. 208; & D.N.B.

Hickman, Thomas, of Coventry, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 16; B.A. 17 Dec., 1577, clerk of Magdalen Coll. 1576-79; perhaps rector of Upton Scudamore, Wilts, 1579. See Bloxam, ii. 42; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Stephens, Thomas (Stevens), pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 12, B.A. 5 March, 1577-8; perhaps bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1585, as 3s. Edward, of Horsley, co. Gloucester, gent. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Webb, John, of Wilts, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 15, B.A. supd. 13 March, 1580-1; perhaps rector of Melbury Osmond, Dorset, 1580, or vicar of Ogbourne St. George's, Wilts, 1589. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Shelberie, John, of Middx., pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 14; student of Lincoln's Inn 1590, as of London, gent.; perhaps M.P. West Looe circa Feb.-April, 1593. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Waterhouse, Philip, of Yorks, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date (1574 ?), aged 16; B.A. from University Coll. 17 Dec., 1577, M.A. 7 April, 1581; brother of Stephen.
Burr, Mark, of Kent, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 22; vicar of Eynsford, Kent, 1579. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Kinge, Andrew, of London, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 15; B.A. 27 June, 1577, M.A. 7 July, 1581.
Porter, Anthony, of co. Gloucester, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 18.
Woodwarde, Richard, of co. Warwick, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 18.
Adderley, Ralph, of co. Lincoln, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 16.
Evetts, John, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 16.
Halton, John, of co. Worcester, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 13.
Healey, George (Helie), of co, Lincoln, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date (1574?), aged 19.
Lowndes, Thomas, of Cheshire, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 23.
Thomas, Peers ap, of co. Anglesea, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date circa 1574, aged 20.
Dunnell, Nicholas, of Hants, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 18.
Batner, Richard, of Sussex, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 15.
Newman, William, of Bucks, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 18.
Ourrey, Thomas, of co. Lincoln, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date circa 1574, aged 17.
Mawor, Peter, of co. Lincoln, pleb. Magdalen Hall., matric. entry under date circa 1574; aged 17.
Arden, Evans, of Oxon, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5, aged 20.
Michell, Francis, of Essex, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date circa 1574, aged 18.
Good, William, of Dorset, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date (1574?), aged 15.
Wighte, Thomas, of Coventry, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 1574, aged 19.
Brinde, John, of Wilts, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date (? 1574), aged 12.
Royse, Robert, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 10 Jan., 1574-5.

Tate, (Sir) William, of Northants, gen. fil. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 22 Dec., 1576, aged 17; of Delapre, Northants, bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1587 (as son of Bartholomew, of Coventry), M.P. Corfe Castle 1593, Northants 1614, knighted 2 Feb., 1605-6; died 1617; father of John, Zouch, and of William next-named. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Veale, Thomas, of co. Lincoln, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 22 Dec., 1576, aged 16, B.A. 2 April, 1582, M.A. 11 July, 1584; incorporated at Cambridge 1586; rector of Mablethorpe St. Mary, co. Lincoln, 1586. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Jen(n)ings, Samuel, of London, pleb., of Magdalen Hall in or before 1572, aged 14; demy Magdalen Coll. 1576-85, B.A. 3 Nov., 1582, M.A. 6 July, 1585, canon of Wells 1585, rector of Hazleton, co. Gloucester, 1586. See Bloxam, iv. 200; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Spurway, Edward, of Devon, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 22 Dec., 1576, aged 18; fellow All Souls' Coll. 1578, B.C.L. 11 Nov., 1585, D.C.L. 23 Jan., 1594-5; rector of Browne-Candover, Hants, 1595. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Wisse, Henry, of co. Gloucester, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 22 Dec., 1576, aged 13; demy Magdalen Coll. 1583-9, B.A. 12 Nov., 1584; 2nd son of Thomas, of Thornbury, co. Gloucester, and brother of Thomas. See Bloxam, iv. 223.

Hornbye, Henry, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Magdalen Hall., matric. entry under date 22 Dec., 1576, aged 17; "Oxoniam venit 11 July, 1575," B.A. 16 Dec., 1581, vicar of Quinton, co. Gloucester, 1593. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Mainwaring, Edward (Maynewaringe), of Salop, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 22 Dec., 1576, aged 19; student of Inner Temple 1579, as of Ightfield, Salop (son of Sir Arthur). See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Saintlowe, John (Saynlowe), of Somerset, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 22 Dec., 1576, aged 17; student of Middle Temple 1579, as of Staunton, Somerset, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Kinnersley, Nicholas, of co. Lincoln, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 22 Dec., 1576, aged 18; student of Inner Temple 1577, as of North Witham, co. Lincoln. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Grover, Simon, of Bucks, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 22 Dec., 1576, aged 19; B.A. 17 Dec., 1577, M.A. 15 June, 1580, rector of Kentchurch, co. Hereford, 1595. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Dayrell, William, of Magdalen Hall 1572; B.A. 29 March, 1576, fellow of Magdalen Coll. 1576 (3rd son of Paul), inventory at Oxford dated 20 Sept., 1577, brother of Francis 1572, and of Walter 1579.
Basden, Thomas, of Kent, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 22 Dec., 1576, aged 18; demy Magdalen Coll. 1581-6 (as Busden), see Bloxam, iv. 223), B.A. 3 Nov., 1582, M.A. 22 June, 1587.
Wright, Ralph, of co. Derby, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 22 Dec., 1576, aged 12; bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1590, as of Snelston, co. Derby. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Simpkins, John, of Bucks, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 22 Dec., 1576, aged 18; called Smile? in O.H.S. xi. 72, and may be Smilius or Smifius. See also Bloxam, ii. 43.
Harries, James, of Somerset, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 22 Dec., 1576, aged 17; one or these names rector of Saltford, Somerset, 1582. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Merry, Henry (Merye), of co. Derby, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 22 Dec., 1576, aged 16; one of these names knighted 20 Aug., 1621.
Wisse, Thomas, of co. Gloucester, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 22 Dec., 1576, aged 18; son and heir of Thomas, of Thornbury.
Preston, Rowland, of Salop, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 22 Dec., 1576, aged 9; brother of William same date.
Lawson, William (Lauson or Lanson), of co. Lincoln, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 22 Dec., 1576, aged 19.
Preston, William, of Salop, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 22 Dec., 1576, aged 18; brother of Rowland.
Forde, Thomas (Fourde), of co. Stafford, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 22 Dec., 1576, aged 31.
Stephens, Richard (Stevens), of Surrey, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 22 Dec., 1576, aged 14.
Lactous, James, of Ghent, in Flanders, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 22 Dec., 1576, aged 17.
Roe, Thomas (Rooe), of co. Stafford, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 22 Dec., 1576, aged 17.
Trimwell, Thomas, of co. Worcester, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 22 Dec., 1576, aged 19.
Mo(o)re, Richard, of Southants, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 22 Dec., 1576, aged 16.
Cocke, Robert, of co. Lincoln, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 22 Dec., 1576, aged 18.
Castorllus, John, of Geneva, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 22 Dec., 1576, aged 22.
Bisse, Thomas, of Somerset, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 22 Dec., 1576, aged 17.
Mabancke, John, of Surrey, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 22 Dec., 1576, aged 14.
Barnabie, John, of Bucks, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 22 Dec., 1576, aged 15.
Parker, Roger, of Wilts, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 22 Dec., 1576, aged 14.
Weaver, John, of Salop, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 22 Dec., 1576, aged 18.

Abdie, Edmund, of London, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 16; of Thavies Inn, bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1589, invited to the bench 1604, grandfather of Christopher 1650. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Allen, David, of Oxon, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 17; B.A. 5 April, 1582, vicar of Brixworth, Northants, 1583, rector of Ludborough, co. Lincoln, 1594. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Samborne, (Sir) Barnabas, of Somerset, gen. fil. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 17; of Timsbery, Somerset (s. John), knighted 23 July, 1603, and died in 1610; father of William 1624.

Brande, Christopher, of co. Lincoln, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 16; clerk of Magdalen Coll. 1585-94, B.A. 22 Feb., 1583-4, M.A. (sup. 30 June) 1589. See Bloxam, ii. 44.
Locke, Zachary, of London, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 18; B.A. March, 1579-80, M.P. Ipswich 1593, and Southwark 1601. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.
Dawes, William (Daws), of Middlesex, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 18; B.A. 15 July, 1580, M.A. 7 May, 1583 (? scholar of Balliol in 1575).
Foxe, Anthony, of London, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 14; B.A. 23 Jan., 1580-1, student of Gray's Inn 1582. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Dayntithe, Charles, of Notts, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 20, vicar of Kirk Ella, Yorks, 1592. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Joyce, Robert, of Kent, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 18; vicar of Rainham, Kent, 1593. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Maye, Thomas, of Somerset, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 17; student of Gray's Inn 1579. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Blake, William, of Somerset, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 17; B.A. 16 March, 1580-1, M.A. 2 June, 1584, as Blicke.
Cæsar, Charles, of London, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 16; 'an eminent soldier,' brother of Julius 1575.
Portington, George, of Yorks, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 16; B.A. from St. Mary Hall 6 July, 1581.
Lodington, William, of co. Lincoln, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 17; B.A. 27 May, 1579.
Austine, Nicholas, of co. Gloucester. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 20. See O.H.S., i.
Tinborowe, John (Tynborowe), of Wilts, pleb. Magdalen Hall. matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 17.
Puller, William, of co. Worcester, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 18.
Gennis, Solomon, of Oxon, cler. fil. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 13.
Hanwell, John, of co. Warwick, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 18.
Samborne, Israel, of Somerset, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 16.
Daniel, William, of Wilts, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 17.
Fountaine, John, of Bucks, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 13.

Angell, Michael, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 7 July, 1578, aged 14; one of these names vicar of Neverne, co. Pembroke, 1600, preb. of Llandegwy, in Brecon, 1601, and of Llangan, in St. David's, 1601, vicar of Nantmel, co. Radnor, 1610. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Oldisworth, Arnold, of co. Gloucester, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 7 July, 1578, aged 17; of Bradley, co. Gloucester, a student of Lincoln's Inn 1580, an associate to the bench of that inn 1612, keeper of the Hanaper, M.P. Tregony 1593, Cirencester 1640-11. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Champnes, Justinian, of Kent, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric-entry under date 7 July, 1578, aged 16; of Wrotham, Kent, bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1599 (as son and heir of Justinian, of Bexley, Kent, arm.), buried at Wrotham 2 March, 1622. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Albany, Robert, of London, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 7 July, 1578, aged 17; fellow All Souls' Coll. 1579, some time of Furnival's Inn, admitted to Lincoln's Inn, 1582, knighted at Hampton Court 22 May, 1605. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Higgons, Richard, of Sussex, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 7 July, 1578, aged 13; bencher of Gray's Inn 1619, as of Burye, Sussex (s. Edward), a justice of the peace and living 1634; brother of William 1578. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Harries, Brian (Harryes), of co. Hereford, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 7 July, 1578, aged 18, rector of Cherrington, co. Gloucester, 1594; father of Timothy 1627. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Baylyes, Thomas, of co. Worcester, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 7 July, 1578, aged 16; a student of Lincoln's Inn 1583, formerly of Lyon's Inn. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Higgons, William, of Sussex, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 7 July, 1578, aged 14; demy Magdalen Coll. 1583-6, B.A. 12 Nov., 1584; brother of Richard 1578. See Bloxam, iv. 223.
Wythe, Thomas, of co. Worcester, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 7 July, 1578, aged 16; student of Inner Temple 1581. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Foxley, John, of Northants, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 7 July, 1578, aged 17; student of Lincoln's Inn 1582. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Ernley, Francis, of Wilts, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 7 July, 1578, aged 18; perhaps 3rd son of John, of Cannings, Wilts.
Carrick, Henry (Carique), of co. Gloucester, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 7 July, 1578, aged 16.
Feild, Robert (Field or Fyelde), of Herts, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 7 July, 1578, aged 17.
Oldisworth, Thomas, of co. Gloucester, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 7 July, 1578, aged 14.
Pressey, Samuel (Presey), of Wilts, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 7 July, 1578, aged 17.
Angell, Robert, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 7 July, 1578, aged 15.
Howse, Robert (House), of London, gen. Magdalen Hall., matric. entry under date 7 July, 1578, aged 16.
Lathburye, Francis, of co. Derby, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 7 July, 1578, aged 18.
Eglington, Francis, of Bucks, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 7 July, 1578, aged 17.
Coulson, John, of Somerset, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 7 July, 1578, aged 17.
Hunt, Philip, of Berks, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 7 July, 1578, aged 17.

Hyde, (Sir) Laurence, of Wilts. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date circa 1579, aged 17 (as gen.), B.A. 9 March, 1579-80; of Heale, near Salisbury, bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1589, treasurer 1616 (as 2nd son of Laurence, of West Hatch, Wilts), M.P. Heytesbury 1584-5, 1597-8, Chippenham 1586-7, Marlborough 1601, 1604-11, knighted 27 Nov., 1614, attorney to Anne, queen of James I.; died 26 Jan., 1641; brother of Nicholas 1590, and of Henry 1579; father of Alexander 1615, Edward 1625, Laurence and Robert 1610, William 1613, Thomas 1627, Francis 1616, and James 1632. See Foster's Judges and Barristers; Fasti, i. 212; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Hyde, Henry, of Wilts, gen. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date circa 1579, aged 16; B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 22 Nov., 1581, M.A. 10 Dec., 1584; of Middle Temple 1585 (as 3rd son of Lawrence, of West Hatch, Wilts, esq.), M.P. Ludgershall 1588-9, Old Sarum 1601; brother of Laurence 1579, and of Nicholas 1590, etc., and father of Edward 1623, and of the next-named. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Tomlins, Richard, of co. Gloucester, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 9 April, 1579, aged 18; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1589 (as son and heir of Richard, of Toddenham, co. Gloucester, gent.); probably M.P. Ludlow in 5 parliaments 1621-2, 1624-5, 1625, 1626, and 1628-9. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Palmer, John, of co. Gloucester, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 9 April, 1579, aged 17; B.A. from Queen's Coll. 26 Jan., 1579-80, M.A. 14 June, 1583; student of Middle Temple 1582, as son and heir of Richard, of Bourton-onthe-Hill, co. Gloucester. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Pusey, John, of Bucks, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 9 April, 1579, aged 17; demy Magdalen Coll. 1579-86, B.A. 12 Nov., 1584, fellow 1586-1610, M.A. 10 July, 1590, B.D. 12 July, 1600, D.D. sup. 7 July, 1608; died 1611. See Bloxam, iv. 222.

Palmer, Robert, of co. Gloucester, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 9 April, 1579, aged 13; student of Middle Temple 1584, as 2s. Richard, of Bourton-on-the-Hill, co. Gloucester. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Keele, Sebastian, of Bucks, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 9 April, 1579, aged 15; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1589, as son and heir of Thomas, of Chipping Wycombe, Bucks. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Grove, John, of Dorset, arm. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 9 April, 1579, aged 17; student of Middle Temple 1582, as son and heir of William, of Shaftesbury, Dorset; died 1620. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Hilpe, John, of co. Gloucester, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 9 April, 1579, aged 16; a student of Inner Temple 1584, and of Thornbury, co. Gloucester. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Fullner, Nathaniel (or Fullver), of Middlesex, arm. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 9 April, 1579, aged 17; a student of Gray's Inn 1580, as Fullware. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Waterhouse, Stephen, of Yorks, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 9 April, 1579, aged 20; B.A. from University Coll. 5 July, 1581, M.A. 27 June, 1583; brother of Philip.
Cocks, Seth, of co. Gloucester, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 9 April, 1579, aged 17. See also Coxe & Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Holliday, Thomas, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 9 April, 1579, aged 17.
Banister, Thomas, of co. Worcester, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 9 April, 1579, aged 18.
Starkey, Francis, of co. Derby, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 9 April, 1579, aged 17.
Searson, John, of co. Lincoln, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 9 April, 1579, aged 18.
Gee, Nathaniel, of London, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 9 April, 1579, aged 15.

Tinley, Robert (Tynlie), of Kent, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 25 Feb., 1580-1, aged 19, fellow Magdalen Coll. 1582, B.A. 3 Nov., 1582, M.A. 22 June, 1587, proctor 1595; licenced to preach 13 March, 1595-6, B.D. 15 Dec., 1597, D.D. 10 July, 1600; vicar of Witham, Essex, 1607-16, archdeacon 1600, and canon of Ely 1603, canon of St. Paul's 1608, rector of Glemsford, Suffolk, 1602, and of Duxford St. Peter 1601, and rector of Cottenham, co. Cambridge, 1602, until his death in 1616. See Ath. ii. 191; Lansdowne MS. 983, f. 281; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Parkehurst, John, of Surrey, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 25 Feb., 1580-1 aged 17; demy Magdalen Coll. 1583-88, B.A. 12 Nov., 1584, fellow 1588-1603, M.A. 10 July, 1590, proctor 1597, B.D. 22 Nov., 1600 (incorporated at Cambridge 1609), D.D. 6 July, 1610, master of Balliol Coll. 1617-37 (2s. Henry, of Guildford), rector of Shillingford, Berks, 1602-39, and of Newington, Oxon, 1619-39, and of Little Wakering, Essex, 1623, until his death 1639. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Bloxam, iv. 223.

Hubbucke, William, of co. Durham, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 19; B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 17 Feb., 1580-1; scholar and fellow Corpus Christi Coll. 1581, M.A. 9 June, 1585, incorporated at Cambridge, 1586, chaplain Tower of London 1595, rector of Nailstone, co. Leicester, 1598; father of John 1620. See Cooper, ii. 528; Ath. i. 752; D.N.B.; & Remembrancia, 442.

Bucke, (Sir) John, of co. Worcester. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 25 Feb., 1580-1, aged 15 (as gent.); of Hamby Grange, co. Lincoln, a student of the Inner Temple 1586, M.P. Droitwich 1601, (son of Francis, of Nash), knighted 23 July, 1603, and died in 1648, father of Creswell and Gervase, and brother of Francis. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Baber, John, of Somerset, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 16, B.A. 25 Oct., 1583, M.A. from Lincoln's Coll. 10 Dec., 1586; B.D. 11 Feb., 1593-4, D.D. 30 Jan., 1595-6, preb. of Exeter 1590, vicar of Chew Magna 1589, rector of Tormarton, co. Gloucester, 1591, father of the next named. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Allibond, Peter, of Oxon, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 20; B.A. 21 Feb., 1581-2, M.A. 6 July, 1585, rector of Cheneys, Bucks, 1592, until his death 6 March, 1628-9, buried in the chancel, father of the last named and of the next named. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Ath., ii. 440.

Brereton, John, of Cheshire, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 27, perhaps of Burras, co. Denbigh, and late of Clifford's Inn, gent., a student of the Inner Temple 21 Feb., 1578-9, who had licence 26 June, 1585, to marry Margaret, relict of Robert Kempton. See Marriage Licences, ed. Foster.

Upham, Thomas (Uppam), of Devon, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 25 Feb., 1580-1, aged 20; fellow Exeter Coll. 1583-92, B.A. 10 Nov., 1586, M.A. 30 June, 1589, as Upham; rector of East Worlington, Devon, 1591-1603, and vicar of Plymouth St. Andrew at his death in 1603. See Boase, 48; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Selwyne, Jasper (Sellwyne), of co. Gloucester, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 19; of Matesden, co. Gloucester, (2s. William, of Stanley Regis, co. Gloucester); bar.at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1591; treasurer 1620; buried 1634-5; father of William 1607. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Foster, (Sir) Guy, of Bucks, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 25 Feb., 1580-1, aged 17; student of Gray's Inn 1583 (as son and heir of Edmund, of Hanslape, Bucks), living in 1634, father of William Forster 1639. See Lipscombe's Bucks, iv. 174; & Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Busted, William, of co. Gloucester, 'cler. fil.' Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 17; B.A. from St. Edmund Hall 13 Nov., 1591, M.A. 21 Nov., 1595, B.D., 18 Jan., 1609-10, rector of Stow-on-the-Wold 1603. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Mathewe, Samuel, of Middlesex, cler. fil. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 25 Feb., 1580-1, aged 17; B.A. from St. Edmund Hall, 13 May, 1585 (s. Toby, bishop of Durham); buried in St. Mary Minor, Cambridge, 17 June, 1601; brother of Toby 1590.

Smithe, Ambrose, of Rutland, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 16, B.A. 25 Oct., 1583, incorporated at Cambridge 1585; perhaps student of Gray's Inn 1588, as of Withicote, co. Leicester. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Shellie, Henry, of Bucks, arm. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 25 Feb., 1580-1, aged 17; perhaps of Warminghurst, Sussex, M.P. Steyning, 1586-7, Bramber 1604-11; died Dec., 1623, father of the next. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Pr(i)estleye, John, of Yorks, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 19, B.A. 6 Feb., 1582-3, bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1593, as of Soyland, Yorks. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Dethicke, William, of co. Derby, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 17; B.A. from Merton Coll. 7 Feb., 1582-3, of Gray's Inn 1586, as of London, gent. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Heblethwayte, William, of London, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 17; B.A. 6 Feb., 1582-3, vicar of Alkborough, Yorks, 1626, until his death 1645. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Horne, Robert, of Newcastle, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 25 Feb., 1580-1, aged 16; B.A. (?7) Feb., 1583-4, M.A. 6 July, 1587, chaplain Magdalen Coll. 1585-95. See Bloxam, ii. 129; & D.N.B.

Weston, Richard, of co. Gloucester, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 25 Feb., 1580-1, aged 17; student of Inner Temple 1582, as of Lichfield (s. Sir James). See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Browning, Richard (Browening), of co. Gloucester, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 25 Feb., 1580-1, aged 16; probably of Cowley, co. Gloucester, and son of John.
Gifford, Henry (Gyffard), of Middlesex, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 20; student of Lincoln's Inn 1584. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Baughe, Edward, of co. Gloucester, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 18; a student of Lincoln's Inn 1583. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Willis, Robert, of co. Worcester, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 19; B.A. from Brasenose Coll. 29 Oct., 1583; fellow Oriel Coll. 1584.
Chadwell, Simon, of Oxon, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 25 Feb., 1580-1, aged 15; of Lincoln's Inn 1586. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Webbe, William, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 17, B.A. from Broadgates Hall 8 Feb., 1582-3.
Robinson, Thomas, of Cheshire, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 25 Feb., 1580-1, aged 21; B.A. from Brasenose Coll. 30 Jan., 1582-3.
Dudson, Elias, of co. Lancaster, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 20; B.A. from Balliol Coll. 1 July, 1583.
Hickman, William (Hickeman), of Oxon, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 20; B.A. 28 Feb., 1583-4.
Ashton, Richard, of Hants, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 25 Feb., 1580-1, aged 20. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Moasie, Christopher, of Oxon, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 18; called Measie by Chester.
Clutterbuck, Richard (Cloterbooke), of co. Gloucester, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 17.
Lye, Richard, of co. Leicester, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 20; B.A. 6 Feb., 1582-3.
Standishe, Stephen, of Bucks, arm. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 25 Feb., 1580-1, aged 14, B.A. 15 Dec., 1586.
Ebden, George, of co. Cambridge, arm., Doctoris fil. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 20.
Aubrey, Nicholas (Aubrye), of co. Warwick, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 25 Feb., 1580-1, aged 10.
Hale, George, of Oxon, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 19; B.A. 6 Feb., 1582-3.
Boughton, George, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 25 Feb., 1580-1, aged 18.
Gre(e)ning, John, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 18.
Robinson, Francis, of Northants, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 25 Feb., 1580-1, aged 15.
Clarke, Samuel, of co. Warwick, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 18.
Flamell, Henry, of co. Warwick, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 17.
Nott, James, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 17.
Phipen, Hercules, of Somerset, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 19.
Dalimuntt, Henry, of Norwich, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 18.
Portington, Robert, of Yorks, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 19.
Wayte, John, of co. Stafford, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 20.
Lowse, William, of Somerset, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 20.
Prichard, Hugh ap, of Wales, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 18.
Sexten, Lawrence, of Hants, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 25 Feb., 1580-1, aged 13.
Beste, Richard, of London, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 25 Feb., 1580-1, aged 17.
Bourne, Richard, of London, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 15.
Petipher, Anthony, of Oxon, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 18.
Legingham, John, of Bucks, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 16.
Walles, John, of Somerset, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 17.
Bourne, Thomas, of Kent, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 17.
Foxe, Michael, of Oxon, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 25 Feb., 1580-1, aged 13.
Barker, John, of Berks, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 17.
Watts, Robert, of Oxon, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 20.
Alce, John, of Sussex, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric.-entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 18.
Lorde, Henry, of Oxon, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 17.
Harte, John, of Kent, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry under date 15 April, 1580, aged 17.

Daniell, Samuel, of Somerset, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 17 Nov., 1581, aged 19; 'the most noted poet and historian of his time,' gentleman extraordinary and one of the grooms of the privy chamber to Anne, Queen Consort of James I., died Oct., 1619, buried in Beckington Church, made his will 4 Sept., 1619, proved 1 Feb., following, his brother John a musician of eminence, sole executor. See John, 1596; Ath., ii. 268; & D.N.B.

Smith, Roger, of Kent, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 17 Nov., 1581, aged 16; chorister Magdalen Coll. 1575, B.A. 17 Feb., 1584-5, clerk 1585-9, master of the school 1586-94; one of these names vicar of Weedon, Northants, 1605, until expelled by the parliamentary committee 1647; buried there 6 June, 1651. See Foster's Graduati Cantab. & Bloxam, ii. 46.

Newton, William, of Sussex, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 17 Nov. 1581, aged 17; bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1592, bencher 1613 (s. William, of the Priory of St. Pancras at Southover, Sussex, formerly of Cheshire); died 24 May, 1648, aged 84, buried in Southover church. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Burton, (Sir) Edward, of Sussex. Magdalen Hall, matric. 24 Nov., 1581 (as pleb.), aged 16; student of Gray's Inn 1587, as of Borne, Sussex, gent. (son of John), brother of Nicholas and of Pelham same date, and father of the next named and of Nicholas 1625. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Leonarde, William, of Northants, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 29 Nov., 1581, aged 16; B.A. 25 Oct., 1583; M.A. from Gloucester Hall 8 July, 1586, B.D. and licenced to preach 7 March, 1606-7, D.D. 12 July, 1608, rector of Kirkhampton, Cornwall, 1611, &c. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Overton, Valentyne, of Middx., pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 17 Nov., 1581, aged 16; B.A. 30 March, 1587, M.A. 9 July, 1590, canon of Lichfield 1593, archdeacon of Derby 1603. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Bowman, Gabriel, of Surrey, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 24 Nov., 1581, aged 19; B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 6 Feb., 1583-4, clerk 1585-9; M.A. from Magdalen Hall 28 June, 1587. See Bloxam, ii. 45.
Crowther, John, of London, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 24 Nov., 1581, aged 17 (subscribes as of Magdalen College); of Gray's Inn 1583, as son of George, of London. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Roots, Thomas, of Sussex, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry 17 Nov., 1581, aged 13; B.A. supd. 1 July, 1584, bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1594. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Burton, Nicholas, of Sussex, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 24 Nov., 1581, aged 11; demy Magdalen Coll. 1586-93, brother of Edward and Pelham same date. See Bloxam, iv. 226.
Robins, Henry, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 29 Nov., 1581, aged 21; bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1591, bencher 1609. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Woolbe, Richard, of co. Lincoln, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 24 Nov., 1581, aged 16; student of Gray's Inn 1588, as of Thorpe, co. Lincoln. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Gavell, Edmund, of Surrey, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 24 Nov., 1581, aged 20 (christian name "Edward" in Mat. Reg.). See Manning and Bray's Surrey, ii. 734.
Burton, Pelham, of Sussex, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 24 Nov., 1581, aged 15; brother of Edward and Nicholas same date.
Dryden, David, of Northants, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 17 Nov., 1581, aged 21; B.A. 9 July, 1584, M.A. 28 June, 1587.
Stockwell, William, of co. Warwick, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 23 March, 1581-2, aged 18, B.A. 9 July, 1585.
Metcalfe, Cornelius, of Bucks, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 24 Nov., 1581, aged 16. See Zaccheus.
Cromwell, Richard (Crumwell), of Wilts, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 23 March, 1581-2, aged 13.
Mosocke, Henry, of co. Lancaster, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 24 Nov., 1581, aged 18.
Page, Thomas, of Middlesex, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry 17 Nov., 1581, aged 14.
Urlen, Roger, of Middlesex, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry 17 Nov., 1581, aged 12.
Francklin, Joseph, of Somerset, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 1 Feb., 1581-2, aged 18.
Sawer, William, of co. Lincoln, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 17 Nov., 1581, aged 15.
Kinder, John, of co. Lincoln, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 17 Nov., 1581, aged 18.
Banninge, Richard, of Wilts, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 17 Nov., 1581, aged 15.
Gavell, Francis, of Surrey, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 24 Nov., 1581, aged 21.
Tuxwell, John, of Somerset, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 24 Nov., 1581, aged 18.
Daniell, George, of Wilts, arm. Magdalen Hall, matric. 1 Feb., 1581-2, aged 17.
Parker, Francis, of Berks, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 24 Nov., 1581, aged 19.
Diston, Robert, of Wilts, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 17 Nov., 1581, aged 16.
Lucey, William, of Oxon, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 17 Nov., 1581, aged 16.
Massy, Henry, of Wilts, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 24 Nov., 1581, aged 22.

Bridgman, (Sir) John, of co. Gloucester, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 22 June, 1582, aged 13; of Nympsfield (s. John, of Micheldean, co. Gloucester), bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1600, serjt.-at-law 1623, knighted 7 Dec., 1623, chief justice Court of Session co. palatine, Cheshire, 1625 (as of Sudbury, co. Gloucester, sometime of Clifford's Inn, gent.), died 5 Feb., 1637-8, will dated 28 Nov., 1637, proved 23 March, 1638, father of Thomas, 1625. See Foster's Judges and Barristers & Lancashire Collection.

Bawgh, Rowland, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 11 May, 1582, aged 17; bar-. at-law, Inner Temple, 1608, as of Twyning, co. Gloucester, licenced, 24 Oct., 1590, to marry Jane, daughter of John Hill, late of St. Michael le Querne, London, 'pellipharius' dec., another licence 2 Sept., 1608 (aged 21, and a bachelor), to marry Jane, daughter of Matthew Isham, of Hampstead, spr. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Geere, John (Geare), of Dorset, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 1 Feb., 1582-3, aged 16, B.A. 6 July, 1588; M.A. from Queen's Coll. 4 July, 1594, vicar of Lyme Regis, Dorset, 1609, and rector of Symondsbury, Dorset, which "he cannot enjoy by reason of the enemies' forces lying in those parts" (1645); dead before 27 Dec. 1645. See Foster's Index Eccl.; & Add. MS. 15,669, p. 73.

Wolveridge, (Sir) James, of Hants, pleb. fil. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry 9 Nov., 1582, aged 13; of Odiam, Southants (s. James), bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1596, bencher 1613, a master in chancery 1615-24, knighted 29 June, 1619; died 7 Dec., 1624; brother of the next. See Foster's Judges and Barristers & Berry's Hants, 313.

Hankinson, Samuel, of Middlesex, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 9 Nov., 1582, aged 16; sup. for B.A. from St. Edmund Hall 17 Nov., 1585, vicar of Hillingdon, Middlesex, 1588, rector of Aughton, 1602, and of Huyton, co. Lancaster, 1608, rector of Holy Trinity, Chester, 1613. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Baber, Francis, of London, arm. Magdalen Hall, matric. 6 April, 1582, aged 16; a student of Lincoln's Inn 1586, as of Somerset (his father Edward was a bencher of Lincoln's Inn 1569, and serjeantat-law 1577). See Foster's Inns of Court Reg. & Marriage Licences, ed. Foster.

Hollymann, Leonell, of London, gent. Magdalen Hall*, matric. 18 May, 1582, aged 17; B.A. from Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1585, incorporated as M.A. 9 July, 1594, rector of Boyton, Wilts, 1600; father of the next-named. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Francklynn, John, of Wilts, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 18 May, 1582, aged 18; one of these names M.P. Marlborough 1640 (l.p.), until his death in 1645. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.
Wyatt, William, of Northants, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 30 March, 1582, aged 16; demy Magdalen Coll. 1585-8, B.A. 27 June, 1587, fellow 1588; died 1589. See Bloxam, iv. 224.
Wright, William (Wryght), of Northants, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 9 Nov., 1582, aged 17, bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1595, bencher 1612. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Hodgekinson, Thomas, of co. Leicester, cler. fil. Magdalen Hall, matric. 25 May, 1582, aged 14. perhaps B.A. from St. Mary Hall 26 March, 1591, see next entry.
Bridgeman, Winter, of co. Gloucester. Magdalen Hall, matric. 22 June, 1582, aged 17; of Limerick, Ireland, son of William, of Great Deane, co. Gloucester.
Jackson, George, of co. Warwick, gent. Magdalen Hall., matric. 3 Aug., 1582, aged 16; possibly a student of Gray's Inn 1584. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Bushell, Edward (Busshell), of co. Gloucester, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 9 Nov., 1582, aged 14; perhaps knighted 19 Dec., 1604.
Keeling, Daniel, of Oxon, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 26 Oct., 1582, aged 17; B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 1 Feb., 1585-6.
Pohill, Thomas, of Kent, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry 1 March, 1582-3, aged 13. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Cleaver, Randall (Clever), of co. Warwick, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 14 Sept., 1582, aged 17; B.A. 16 Dec., 1585.
Tynte, Edmond, of Somerset, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 11 May, 1582, aged 18; perhaps brother of John same date.
Wolveridge, John, of Hants, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry 9 Nov., 1582, aged 13; brother of the preceding.
Cable, William, of Somerset, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 9 Nov., 1582, aged 15; called Cabell in Mat. Reg.
Baylye, William, of Devon, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 9 Nov., 1582, aged 23; called Bealye in Mat. Reg.
Bushell, Thomas (Busshell), of co. Gloucester, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 9 Nov., 1582, aged 15.
Deare, Ralph, of Hants, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 14 June, 1582, aged 19; B.A. 11 Feb., 1584-5.
Wincote, Charles, of co. Warwick, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 1 March, 1582-3, aged 15.
Davis, John, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 9 Nov., 1582, aged 18.
Bridger, Henry, of Surrey, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 19 Oct., 1582, aged 19.
Brigges, John, of London, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 9 Nov., 1582, aged 17.

Love, Richard, of Northants, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 19 April, 1583, aged 13; demy Magdalen Coll. 1585-8, B.A. 20 Jan., 1590-1, fellow 1591-1618, M.A. 4 July, 1594, B.D. 20 July, 1604, licenced to preach 1 Dec., 1613, rector of Ardley, Oxon, 1615, etc. See Bloxam, iv. 225; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Onley, (Sir) Edward, of Northants, gen. fil. Magdalen Hall, matric. 19 April, 1583, aged 16; of Catesby, student of Gray's Inn 1585, as of Catesby, Northants, esq. (s. Edward), knighted 23 July, 1603; died 12 Aug., 1638. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Howe, Thomas, of Herts, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 16 Oct., 1583, aged 15; B.A. 17 Feb., 1586-7, M.A. 9 July, 1590, rector of Grendon Underwood, Bucks, 1598; perhaps father of Richard 1634. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Henshawe, Thomas, of Cheshire, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 16 Oct., 1583, aged 23; perhaps son of Edward, of Henshaw, Cheshire; captain in Ireland, slain at Blackwater 14 Aug., 1598.
Bucke, Francis, of co. Worcester, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 7 Feb., 1583-4, aged 16; probably son of Francis, of Nashe, and brother of John 1581.
Courte, Thomas, of co. Warwick, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 19 April, 1583, aged 17; B.A. 18 Jan., 1585-6, M.A. 2 May, 1589.
Franckom, Thomas, of co. Gloucester. Magdalen Hall, matric. 21 June, 1583, aged 16; B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 30 March, 1588.
Hedinge, William, of Bucks, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 15 Nov., 1583, aged 20; sup. for B.A. 13 May, 1587, as Hedding.
Peterson, Peter, of Kent, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 10 May, 1583, aged 16; B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 25 Feb., 1586-7.
Osborne, William, of Somerset, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 16 Oct., 1583, aged 18, B.A. 27 May, 1587.
Baker, Thomas, of Berks, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 19 April, 1583, aged 18; B.A. 15 June, 1586.
Ladbrocke, William, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 7 Feb., 1583-4, aged 14.
Shelborne, William, of Northants, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 7 Feb., 1583-4, aged 16.
Grove, William, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 8 Nov., 1583, aged 15.
Griffin, John, of co. Warwick, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 21 June, 1583, aged 17.
Cooke, Samuel, of Somerset, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 7 Feb., 1583-4, aged 17.
Cox, William, of Wilts, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 16 Oct., 1583, aged 17.

Bagnald, Robert, of co. Stafford, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 4 Dec., 1584, aged 24; B.A. from Balliol Coll. 4 July, 1588, rector of Rode, Somerset, 1589-1616, rector of Hutton, Somerset, 1616. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Clutterbuck, Tobie (Clutterbooke), of co. Gloucester, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 5 March, 1584-5, aged 13; B.A. 10 Feb., 1589-90, M.A. 4 July, 1594, of All Saints' parish, Oxford, M.A., will, etc. (at Oxford), 17 April, 1621.

Barnesley, Thomas, of Oxon, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric., 16 Oct., 1584, aged 18; B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 7 Feb., 1588-9; one of these names rector of Turnaston, co. Hereford, 1604. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Blackwell, Thomas, of Wilts, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 4 Dec., 1584, aged 20, B.A. 13 Feb., 1587-8; M.A. from St. Alban Hall 8 July, 1591, rector of Bishopstrow, Wilts, 1606. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Mayne, James, of co. Hereford, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 4 Dec., 1584, aged 18; perhaps bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1593 (as of Hemel Hempstead, Herts), bencher 1613. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Walter, James, of Canterbury, cler. fil. Magdalen Hall, matric. 4 Dec., 1584, aged 17; B.A. from New Coll. 4 June, 1590; vicar of Bishops Lavington, Wilts, 1592. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Wilder, Nicholas, of Berks, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 16 Oct., 1584, aged 17, B.A. 20 Feb., 1589-90, M.A. 7 July, 1592; rector of Dunsfold, Surrey, 1606. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Larke, Nicholas, of co. Lincoln, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 5 March, 1584-5, aged 16; rector of Waddington, co. Lincoln, 1598-1629. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Scote, Thomas, of Yorks, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 26 Feb., 1584-5, aged 17; B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 28 Nov., 1588; M.A. from Magdalen Hall 3 July, 1593.
Miles, Alexander, of Northants, "cond. gen." Magdalen Hall, matric. 19 June, 1584, aged 17; student of Lincoln's Inn 1585. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Partridge, Anthony, of co. Gloucester, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 16 Oct., 1584, aged 15; student of Lincoln's Inn 1587. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Estcourte, Edmund, of co. Gloucester, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 5 March, 1584-5, aged 16. See Rudder's Gloucestershire, 654.
Weston, Roger, of London, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry 5 May, 1584-5, aged 13; B.A. from Trinity Coll. 3 June, 1589.
Seyerd, William (Saiarde), of Somerset, "pleb. fil. gen. cond." Magdalen Hall, matric. 5 March, 1584-5, aged 16.
Clutterbuck, John (Clutterbooke), of co. Gloucester, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 5 March, 1584-5, aged 14.
Fynes, John, of Sussex, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 16 Oct., 1584, aged 16. See Berry's Sussex, 331.
Atterbury, Lewis (Atterberye), of Northants, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 5 March, 1584-5, aged 17.
Browne, Richard, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 4 Dec., 1584, aged 13.
Clark, Richard, of co. Warwick, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 5 March, 1584-5, aged 17.
Marche, Edward, of Kent, "Cond. Gen." Magdalen Hall, matric. 30 Oct., 1584, aged 16.
Jac(k)son, Michael, of Notts, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 16 Oct., 1584, aged 19.
Tucker, Walter, of Devon, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 19 July, 1584, aged 21.

Ellis, Richard, of London, "Gen. Cond." Magdalen Hall., matric. 23 April, 1585, aged 14; B.A. 20 March, 1588-9, M.A. 19 Nov., 1591; one of these names rector of Huish, Devon, 1608, and another rector of Mose, Essex, 1616. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Frith, Thomas, of Kent, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 23 April, 1585, aged 16, B.A. 1 Dec., 1589; M.A. from All Souls' Coll. 10 April, 1594, B.D. 12 Nov., 1605, canon of Windsor 1610-31. See Lansdowne MS. 984, f. 184.
Butler, Alban, of Northants, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 16; son of George Butler, of Aston-le-Walls, Northants, died in 1617.
Locke, Thomas, of Magdalen Hall, matric. 2 July, 1585; possibly bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1600, bencher 1622. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Powell, Daniel, of London, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 14; demy Magdalen Coll. 1586-92. See Bloxam, iv. 226.
Smith, William, of Wilts, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 15; demy Magdalen Coll. 1586-91. See Bloxam, iv. 226.
Sylvester, Henry (Silvester), of Hants, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 5 Nov., 1585, aged 15, B.A. 9 Feb., 1592-3.
Clifford, James, of co. Gloucester, 'Gen. Cond.' Magdalen Hall, matric. 5 Nov., 1585, aged 19.
Dickson, Richard (Dicksing), of Northants, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 16.
Langrishe, Robert, of Southants, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 15.
Andrew, Anthony, of Northants, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 5 Nov., 1585, aged 14.
Butler, Richard, of Northants, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 14.
Butler, Thomas, of Northants, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 12.
Fre(e)man, Thomas, of Oxon, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 23 April, 1585, aged 16.

Charlet, John, of Christ Church, subscribed 21 June, 1583; Magdalen Hall, matric. 16 Feb., 1586-7, aged 19, from co. Worcester, pleb.; B.A. 17 Feb., 1586-7, M.A. 29 Oct., 1590; B.D. from Oriel Coll. 8 July, 1602, licenced to preach 13 May, 1605, D.D. 5 July, 1614, rector of Great Brickhill, Bucks, 1602-8, canon of Worcester 1607, vicar of Overbury, co. Worcester, 1615, rector of Overworton, Oxon, 1620, and of Worcester St. Martin, 1621, vicar of Cropthorne, co. Worcester, 1624, father of Arthur 1639, and father of the next named. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Aylworth, Humphrey (Ailworth), of co. Warwick, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 29 April, 1586, aged 15; B.A. from Broadgates Hall 16 Dec., 1589, rector of St. Mary Bothaw, London, 1602, of Harbledon, Kent, 1601, of Calverton, Bucks, 1604, of Llandyssil, co. Cardigan, 1609, of Tackley, Oxon, 1609, and of Witney 1614. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Jones, William (Johns), of co. Monmouth, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 17 June, 1586, aged 18; B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 26 Feb., 1589-90, and perhaps M.A. from New Inn Hall 6 July, 1593, rector of Bryngwyn, co. Monmouth (as B.A.) 1597. See O.H.S. xii. 158; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Huggins, Robert, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 15 April, 1586, aged 17; sup. for B.A. 6 July, 1590; one of these names vicar of Colerne, Wilts, 1603, rector of St. Stephen, Bristol, 1612, 12th minor canon in St. Paul's 1616. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Newton, Robert, of co. Derby, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry 11 Nov., 1586, aged 14; B.A. from Merton Coll. 4 July, 1590; student of Inner Temple 1591, as of Chaddesden, co. Derby, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Travell, Robert, of Northants, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 29 April, 1586, aged 17, B.A. 27 Oct., 1589, M.A. 7 July, 1592; rector of Weston Favell, Northants, 1593. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Estcourte, Edmund, of Wilts, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 15 April, 1586, aged 15; bar.-atlaw, of Lincoln's Inn, 1598, treasurer 1629. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Cheeke, Robert (Chicke), of Somerset, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 8 July, 1586, aged 14, B.A. 22 June, 1593, M.A. 7 July, 1598. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Parson, Robert, of co. Lincoln, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 17 June, 1586, aged 17; B.A. 26 Feb., 1589-90, M.A. 7 July, 1592.
Cowdale, Edmund, of Bucks, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 17 June, 1586, aged 17; called Edward in Mat. Reg.
Clayton, Nicholas (Cleaton), of co. Warwick, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 8 July, 1586, aged 15.
Holloway, Thomas (Hallowaye), of Bucks, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 17 June, 1586, aged 15.
Berrowe, Edmund, of co. Gloucester, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 24 March, 1586-7, aged 17.
Allen, Griffin (Gressin), of Berks, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 11 Nov., 1586, aged 14.
Seede, Edward, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 15 April, 1586, aged 17.
Ainger, William, of co. Warwick, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 11 Nov., 1586, aged 15.
Newton, William, of Bucks, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry 17 June, 1586, aged 13.
Newton, Francis, of co. Derby, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 11 Nov., 1586, aged 17.
Smith, Thomas (Smyth), of Oxon, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 8 July, 1586, aged 16.
Alford, William, of Yorks, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 29 April, 1586, aged 15.
Ledsam, Thomas, of Wilts, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 11 Nov., 1586, aged 16.
Tucker, Walter, of Devon, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 17 June, 1586, aged 21.
Hill, Robert, of London, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 17 June, 1586, aged 14.
Williams, John, of Oxon, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 17 June, 1586, aged 17.

Will(o)ughby, Robert, of Kent, arm. Magdalen Hall, matric. 23 June, 1587, aged 18; B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 13 Feb., 1592-3, M.A. 8 Feb., 1596-7, B.Med. and supd. for licence to practice medicine 1 March, 1602-3; son of Thomas, of Boreplace, Kent; brother of George same date.
Will(o)ughby, George, of Kent, arm. Magdalen Hall, matric. 23 June, 1587, aged 16, B.A. 2 July, 1591, M.A. 4 July, 1594, son of Thomas, of Boreplace, Kent, brother of Robert same date.
Butler, Samuel, of Oxon, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 1 April, 1587, aged 15; B.A. from University Coll. 15 Nov., 1593, M.A. 28 June, 1596.
Leveridge, John, of Northants, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 19 Jan., 1587-8, aged 13; demy Magdalen Coll. 1589-95. See Bloxam, iv. 230.
Wright, Edward, of Northants, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 13 Jan., 1587-8, aged 18, B.A. 16 April, 1591, M.A. 4 July, 1595.
Jaques, Richard (Jaqius or Jaquius, i.e. Jaque), of Wilts, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 9 Feb., 1587-8, aged 18.
Parker, Thomas, of Middlesex, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 23 June, 1587, aged 15.
Parsonns, Richard, of Kent, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 23 June, 1587, aged 15.
Meeke, Henry, of Stafford, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 23 June, 1587, aged 19.
May, James, of Kent, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 23 June, 1587, aged 17.

Andrew, George, of Northants, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 21 March, 1588-9, aged 13; B.A. 5 May, 1592, M.A. 5 July, 1599, dean of Limerick 1603, precentor of St. Patrick's, Dublin, 1603, bishop of Ferns and Leighlin 1635, preb. of Ferns and of Leighlin 1636, forced to fly on the rebellion of 1641, died Oct., 1648, buried in St. Clement Danes. See Cotton's Fasti Eccl. Hib., ii. 337; & Ath., iv. 806. [Addenda: after 1599; add (2s. Thomas, of Daventry), rector of Dromcliffe, diocese of Killaloe. See Baker's Northants, i. 337.]

Martin, Alexander, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 19 April, 1588, aged 16; demy Magdalen Coll. 1588-90, fellow 1590-1608, B.A. 13 Feb., 1592-3, M.A. 8 Feb., 1596-7, licenced to preach 18 Jan., 1605-6, B.D. 18 March, 1605-6, rector of Shawell, co. Leicester, 1606. See Bloxam, iv. 229; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Bonner, George, of co. Gloucester, gent. Magdalen Hall., matric. 19 April, 1588, aged 17; bar.at-law, Middle Temple, 1602, as 2nd son of Anthony, of Campden, co. Gloucester, gent. deceased. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Hunte, Robert, of Hants, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 14 Feb., 1588-9, aged 20; B.A. 23 Nov., 1592, M.A. 4 July, 1595; one of these names vicar of Reculver, Kent, 1595. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Underhill, John, of co. Warwick, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 8 Nov., 1588, aged 16; demy Magdalen Coll. 1588-96, B.A. 3 Nov., 1595; brother of Edward 1587. See Bloxam, iv. 229.
Mole, George, of Northants, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 8 Nov., 1588, aged 15; student of Inner Temple 1596, as of Segrave, Northants, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Fitzhughes, Thomas (Fitzhues), of co. Gloucester, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 8 June, 1588; aged 18; possibly student of Gray's Inn 1594. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Newarke, John, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 11 Oct., 1588, aged 16, and again 9 May, 1589, aged 15 (sic); B.A. from Balliol Coll. 27 Jan., 1601-2.
Shuttleworthe, Henry, of co. Warwick, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 8 Nov., 1588, aged 15, B.A. 3 July, 1594; student of Gray's Inn 1588. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Keble, Henry (Kible), of co. Gloucr., pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 8 Nov., 1588, aged 17. See Richard 1585.
Follet, Richard, of Devon, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 14 Feb., 1588-9, aged 14; B.A. 10 Nov., 1595.
Fitzhughes, Roger (Fitzhues), of co. Gloucester, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 8 June, 1588, aged 16.
Andrew, Henry, of Northants, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 21 March, 1588-9, aged 15.
Jolliffe, Humphrey, of Dorset, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 7 Feb., 1588-9, aged 13.
Longe, William, of Devon, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 25 Jan., 1588-9, aged 18.
Scott, William, of Yorks, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 25 Jan., 1588-9, aged 18.
Coxhed, Raynould, of Hants, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 8 Nov., 1588, aged 13.
Powell, Roger, of Somerset, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 8 June, 1588, aged 17.

Langton, William, of co. Lincoln, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 9 May, 1589, aged 15; fellow Magdalen Coll., B.A. 13 Feb., 1592-3, M.A. 8 Feb., 1596-7, licenced to preach 5 Dec., 1605, B.D. 18 March, 1605-6, D.D. 6 July, 1610, president of his college 1610-26 (s. John, of Langton, co. Lincoln), rector of Lympsham, Somerset, 1607-15, and of Brightwell, Berks, 1615, until his death 10 Oct., 1626, aged 54; buried in the chapel; brother of John 1576. See Gutch, i. 331; Lansdowne MS. 984, f. 75; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Earburye, Antony, of Wilts, pleb., Magdalen Hall, matric. 10 Oct., 1589, aged 18; B.A. from Merton Coll. 5 Feb., 1591-2, M.A. 12 Nov., 1594. prebendal rector of Wherwell, Hants, 1603-1635, rector of Weston Zoyland, Somerset, 1617, until his death in 1638, father of Mathias. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Loftus, Thomas, of London (city), pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 9 May, 1589, aged 16; demy Magdalen Coll. 1590-7, B.A. 8 Feb., 1593-4, M.A. 17 Oct., 1598, fellow 1599-1617, B.D. 27 Oct., 1608; will at Oxford proved 23 June, 1617. See Bloxam, iv. 231.

Henninge, John, of Dorset, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 10 Oct., 1589, aged 18; B.A. from St. Alban Hall 31 May, 1592, a student of Middle Temple 1592, as son and heir of John, of Nash Court, Dorset, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Register.

Arden, William (Ardynn), of Somerset, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 13 March, 1589-90, aged 19; B.A. 4 Dec., 1592, M.A. 4 July, 1595, rector of Lufton, Somerset, 1596, and of Brimpton 1613. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Earle, Thomas, of Wilts, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 27 Feb., 1589-90, aged 18; B.A. 7 Feb., 1593-4, M.A. 10 June, 1597, vicar of Kemble, Wilts, 1620, father of the next-named. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Partridge, Edward (Partherick), of Kent, arm. Magdalen Hall, matric. 10 Oct., 1589, aged 15; student of Inner Temple 1594, as of Eridge, Kent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Pearse, Edward, of co. Worcester, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 30 Jan., 1589-90, aged 18; perhaps B.A. from St. Edmund Hall 15 July, 1597, as Edmund Peirce, supl. as Edward.
Slywright, Thomas, of Kent, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 25 Oct., 1589, aged 15; bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1606, as of Tenterden, Kent. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Eyre, John, of Wilts, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 29 Jan., 1589-90, aged 15; B.A. from Broadgates Hall 12 June, 1600; called Tyre in O.H.S. xi. 174.
Caye, Abraham, of co. York, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 31 Oct., 1589, aged 14; B.A. 8 Dec., 1593, as Caius. [Addenda: of Lincoln's Inn, 1599.]
Bingham, Henry, of Dorset, militis fil. Magdalen Hall, matric. 10 Oct., 1589, aged 16; probably son of Sir George.
Arnold, John (Arnall), of Notts, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 13 March, 1589-90, aged 13.
Whitakers, Benjamin, of Sussex, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 13 March, 1589-90, aged 17.
Woodroff, Gabriel, of Norfolk, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 10 Oct., 1589, aged 16.
Dormer, John, of Northants, arm. Magdalen Hall, matric. 30 Jan., 1589-90, aged 16.
Whistler, Ralph, of Berks, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 30 Jan., 1589-90, aged 15.
Burdet, William, of Sussex, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 31 Oct., 1589, aged 16.
Mathew, Robert, of Northants, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 9 May, 1589, aged 17.
Rose, Thomas, of Herts, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 10 Oct., 1589, aged 15.

Denison, John, of co. Warwick, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 22 May, 1590, aged 20; B.A. from Balliol Coll. 8 Feb., 1593-4, M.A. 4 July, 1600 (then in orders), licenced to preach 22 Jan., 1605-6, B.D. 23 April, 1607, D.D. 20 June, 1611, domestic chaplain to George, Duke of Buckingham, chaplain to James I., chief moderator free school, Reading, Berks, vicar of St. Mary, Reading, 1593, of St. Laurence, Reading, 1603, and of St. Giles, Reading, 1612, rector of Woodmansterne, Surrey, 1610, until his death, buried there 1 Feb., 1628-9, father of the next named. See Ath., ii. 439; Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus; & D.N.B.

Machon, John, of Yorks, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 26 Feb., 1590-1, aged 19; B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 7 Feb., 1593-4, (s. John, of Machon Banke, near Sheffield), vicar of Aston, co. Warwick, 1603, and of Ridgley, co. Stafford, 1620, canon of Lichfield 1631, master of the hospital of St. John's de Forbrage, in Stafford, 1632, and perhaps vicar of Hartburn, Northants, 1632; died about 1640; father of John 1621. See Foster's Visitation of Durham; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Charlton, Walter (Charelton), pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 22 Jan., 1590-1, aged 16; B.A. from St. Mary Hall 4 June, 1594, M.A. 20 April, 1597, licenced to preach 12 Feb., 1601-2, of Gray's Inn 1591 (as son and heir of Andrew, of Apley, Salop, esq.), vicar of Ilminster, Somerset, 1605, of Somerton 1609, rector of Shepton Mallet 1609, and of Almsford 1610, father of the next named. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Horne, Thomas, of Wilts, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 26 Feb., 1590-1, aged 15; B.A. 19 Oct., 1594; fellow Merton Coll. 1597, M.A. 4 July, 1601, B.D. 4 June, 1616, D.D. 8 July, 1625, canon of Windsor 1616, rector of Methley, Yorks, 1615, vicar of Isleworth, Middlesex, 1622, rector of Farnham Royal, Bucks, 1629; died 4 Nov., 1636. See Lansdowne MS. 984, f. 233; O.H.S. iv. 276; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Gaydon, Peter, of Wilts, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 15 Oct., 1590, aged 16; B.A. 19 Oct., 1594, M.A. 10 June, 1597, rector of South Tidworth, Hants, 1599. See also Peter Gaysdon, and Foster's Index Eccl.
Stanne, Francis, of Oxon, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 23 Oct., 1590, aged 19, B.A. 22 Feb., 1594-5, M.A. 5 July, 1599, then in orders; rector of Chalfield, Wilts, 1598. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Chilcotte, Richard, of Somerset, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 15 Oct., 1590, aged 19; B.A. 19 Oct., 1594, rector of Nettlecombe, Somerset, 1604. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Hall, Nicholas (Haule), of Northants, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 23 Oct., 1590, aged 16; B.A. from Queen's Coll. 28 Jan., 1596-7; M.A. from St. Edmund Hall 11 Feb., 1601-2.
Hinde, Rowland, of London, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 29 Jan., 1590-1, aged 16; student of Lincoln's Inn 1594; brother of William 1591. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Greenewood, Thomas, of Oxford (city), gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 23 Oct., 1590, aged 14, living at Brize Norton, Oxon, in 1634; brother of George, 1594.
Dounbell, Nicholas, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 22 May, 1590, aged 16; B.A. from Gloucester Hall 15 Nov., 1593, M.A. 21 June, 1597.
Sotwell, William, of Wilts, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 19 June, 1590, aged 17; bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1598. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Hinde, Augustin, of London, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 29 Jan., 1590-1, aged 17; student of Lincoln's Inn 1594. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Sutton, John, of Bucks, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 22 Jan., 1590-1, aged 21; B.A. from Queen's Coll. 20 Feb., 1590-1.
Har(e)well, Edward (Harvill), of Kent, cler. fil. Magdalen Hall, matric. 26 Feb., 1590-1, aged 16.
Pawlinge, Thomas (Paulinge), of Oxon, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry 15 Oct., 1590, aged 12.
Baskervile, James, of co. Gloucester, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 22 May, 1590, aged 16.
Twisse, William, of Berks, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry 22 Jan., 1590-1, aged 14.
Jenninges, Giles, of Somerset, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 26 Feb., 1590-1, aged 19.
Raymond, George, of Somerset, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 26 Feb., 1590-1, aged 16.
Dolman, John, of Somerset, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 26 Feb., 1590-1, aged 18.
Parke, John, of co. Lincoln, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 23 Oct., 1590, aged 17.
Marriatt, Edmund, of Berks, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 23 Oct., 1590, aged 16.
Hinton, William, of Oxon, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 15 Oct., 1590, aged 17.
Pawlinge, Thomas, of Oxon, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 22 May, 1590, aged 15.
Hale, Nicholas, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 22 May, 1590, aged 15.
Norris, Morgan, Magdalen Hall, matric. 22 May, 1590, aged 17.

Goffe, Stephen, of Hants, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 22 Oct., 1591, aged 16; demy Magdalen Coll. 1592-8, B.A. 3 Nov., 1595, fellow 1598-1603, M.A. 11 De 5., 1599, "the puritanical minister of Stanmer, Sussex," rector of Bramber 1603, and of St. Botolph 1605, deprived 1607, rector of Stanmer 1603; father of John, of the next-named, of William 1649, and of Nathaniel 1642. See Ath. iii. 525; & Bloxam, iv. 233.

Blencowe, John (Blinkowe), of Northants, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 2 July, 1591, aged 16; a student of the Inner Temple 1594, as of Marston, Northants, late of Clement's Inn, died about 1643, father of George 1620, of Thomas 1620, and of Timothy 1638. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Castillion, Douglas, of Berks, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 18; B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 3 Nov., 1595, M.A. 11 Dec., 1599, rector of Stratford Tony, Wilts, 1619, father of John and of Richard. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Add. MS., 15,671, p. 241.

Manninge, Samuel, of Kent, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 15 Oct., 1591, aged 15; B.A. 28 Feb., 1594-5; student of Inner Temple 1596, as of St. Mary Cray, Kent; brother of Edward 1595. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Kirton, William, of Northants, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 17; a student of Inner Temple 1594, as of Thorpe Mandeville, Northants, gent. (s. Thomas), died s.p. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Talbot, John, of London, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 2 July, 1591, aged 15, B.A. 14 Feb., 1595-6, fellow Merton Coll. 1596, M.A. 8 July, 1600, B.Med. supd. 25 June, 1608. See O.H.S. iv. 276.
Compton, John, of Hants, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 22 Oct., 1591, aged 18; of the Middle Temple 1594, as son and heir of John, of Priors Dean, Hants. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Holled, Bryan, of Northants, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 13; demy Magdalen Coll. 1593-1602, B.A. 27 May, 1597, M.A. 3 July, 1601. See Bloxam, iv. 234.
Milles, Richard (Mylles), of London, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 22 Oct., 1591, aged 16; B.A. 4 July, 1595, as Millar, M.A. 7 July, 1598.
Fountayne, Richard, of Bucks, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 2 July, 1591, aged 17; B.A. 10 Nov., 1595; brother of Francis same date.
Silver, John (Sylver), of co. Warwick, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 2 July, 1591, aged 14, B.A. 20 Feb., 1595-6.
Fountayne, Francis, of Bucks, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 2 July, 1591, aged 14; brother of Richard same date.
Wilkins, Gerrard, of Oxon, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 4 Feb. 1591-2, aged 17, B.A. 28 Feb., 1594-5.
Jones, William, of Wilts, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 22 Oct., 1591, aged 17; B.A. 25 Nov. 1595.
Richardson, William, of co. Worcester, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 22 Oct., 1591, aged 15.
Cotsbrooke, John, of co. Leicester, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 2 July, 1591, aged 15.
Ashley, Henry (Asheley), of Dorset, arm. Magdalen Hall, matric. 2 July, 1591, aged 12.
Jones, Walter, of co. Monmouth, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 22 Oct., 1591, aged 16.
Creswell, John, of co. York, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 16.
Godfrey, Thomas, of Norfolk, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 22 Oct., 1591, aged 14.
Randolphe, William, of Kent, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 22 Oct., 1591, aged 16.
Symons, William, of Hants, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 20.
Milton, James, of Hants, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 17.
Ebden, Paul, of Hants, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 22 Oct., 1591, aged 16.
White, John, of Berks, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 22 Oct., 1591, aged 17.

Robotham, Robert, of Bucks, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 27 Oct., 1592, aged 15, demy 1594-1600, B.A. 27 May, 1597, M.A. 14 Nov., 1600, B. and D.D. 2 July, 1621, vicar of Newland, co. Gloucester, 1601, precentor of Llandaff 1603, rector of St. George-super-Ely, co. Glamorgan, 1603, vicar of Caerwent, co. Monmouth, 1605, archdeacon of Llandaff 1607, rector of Mitcheltroy 1611, and of Llangibby, co. Monmouth, 1611, canon of Hereford 1619, vicar of Dilwyn 1622, and of Aymestry, co. Hereford, 1623; brother of Oliver 1602. See Bloxam, iv. 235; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Samborn, James, of Southants, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 10 Nov., 1592, aged 16, B.A. 20 Feb., 1595-6; rector of Grateley 1604, and of Upper Clatford, Hants, 1610; father of Thomas 1623. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Robotham, Oliver, of Bucks, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 27 Oct., 1592, aged 16, B.A. 16 Dec., 1595; perhaps vicar of Llanedarn, co. Glamorgan, 1617; brother of Robert 1602. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Pigot, Walter, of Salop, arm. Magdalen Hall, matric. 27 Oct., 1592, aged 18; probably of Chetwynd (s. Thomas), sheriff Salop 1624; and father of Thomas and Walter 1623.
Longe, Polydor, of Somerset, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 27 Oct., 1592, aged 16; s. Thomas, of Beckington, Somerset; married and had issue.
Stafford, Thomas, of Bucks, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 27 Oct., 1592, aged 15, demy Magdalen Coll. 1594-7. See Bloxam, iv. 234.
Walton, Toby, of Somerset, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 27 Oct., 1592, aged 18, B.A. 9 July, 1596.
Palmer, Robert, of Yorks, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 3 Nov., 1592, aged 14; B.A. 9 July, 1596.
Nurth, John, of Oxon, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 27 Oct., 1592, aged 17; B.A. 16 Dec., 1595.
Holbache, Thomas, of co. Warwick, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 10 Nov., 1592, aged 23.
Ayer, Thomas, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 27 Oct., 1592, aged 15.
Cox, Thomas, of Wilts, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 10 Nov., 1592, aged 23.

Mansell, Samuel, of Bucks, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 25 Jan., 1593-4, aged 12; B.A. 23 Feb., 1597-8; of Cosgrave, Northants, baptised 15 Sept., 1581, bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1608, as son and heir of John, of Haversham, Bucks, gent. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Wentworth, George, of Yorks, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 1 Feb., 1593-4, aged 18; baptised at Silkstone, Yorks, 25 April, 1576, student of Middle Temple 1596 (as son and heir of Matthew, of Bretton, Yorks, esq.); buried 9 June, 1638. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Folliott, (Sir) John, of co. Worcester. Magdalen Hall, matric. 6 July, 1593, aged 14, as gent. (subscribed same day as Thomas); of Pyrton, co. Worcester (s. Thomas); knighted 10 June, 1603. See Nash's Worcestershire, ii. 258.

Birkhead, William, of co. York, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 1 Feb., 1593-4, aged 16; a student of Lincoln's Inn 1595, one of these names rector of Gamston, Notts, 1597. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Inns of Court Reg.

Long, Gifford, of Wilts, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 6 July, 1593, aged 17; of Rood Ashton, sheriff of Wilts, 1624, M.P. Westbury 1625; made his will in 1635; father of Edward 1623. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Withers, Ralph, of Bucks, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 25 Jan., 1593-4, aged 17, B.A. 16 Nov., 1597, M.A. 8 July, 1600; rector of Upper Isham 1621, and of Orlingbury, (both) Northants, 1621. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Godfrey, Robert, of Norfolk, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 6 July, 1593, aged 15; bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1605. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Folliott, Thomas (Folliote), of co. Worcester, arm. Magdalen Hall, matric. 22 Feb., 1593-4, aged 13; B.A. 23 Feb., 1597-8; brother of Henry and John.
Cooke, John, of London, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 1 Feb., 1593-4, aged 18; of Gray's Inn 1594, as of Westminster. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Smith, Thomas, of Northants, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 22 Feb., 1593-4, aged 17; B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 21 Feb., 1597-8.
Chibnale, Thomas, of Bucks, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 25 Jan., 1593-4, aged 17; B.A. 4 July, 1597, M.A. 6 May, 1602.
Clark, Nicholas, of co. Warwick, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 6 July, 1593, aged 16. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Fitch, Robert (Fytch), of co. Warwick, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 15 June, 1593, aged 17; called Fich in Mat. Reg.
Bosse, John, of Bucks, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 6 July, 1593, aged 15; B.A. 4 July, 1597, M.A. 6 May, 1602.
Fitche, Richard, of co. Warwick, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 15 June, 1593, aged 16; called Fich in Mat. Reg.
Kay, William, of Yorks, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 1 Feb., 1593-4, aged 17, called "Key" in Mat. Reg.
Kinge, Daniel, of co. Leicester, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. Jan., 1593-4, aged 17; B.A. 2 Dec., 1596.
Cooke, Edward, of London, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 1 Feb., 1593-4, aged 17.

Mainwaringe, Edward (Maynwaringe), of co. Stafford, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 8 Nov., 1594, aged 17; of Whitmore, co. Stafford; student of Middle Temple 1595 (as son and heir of Edward, of Whitmore, co. Stafford, esq.), sheriff 1646; M.P. Newcastle-under-Lyme 1625; died 1647. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Mansell, Thomas, of Bucks, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 10 Oct., 1594, aged 14; student of Gray's Inn 1599, as of Chicheley, Bucks, gent., baptised 7 April, 1577, captain in the fleet, ancestor of the Thorpe-Malsor family; brother of John 1592. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Greaves, John, of Notts, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 28 June, 1594, aged 15; B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 28 April, 1598, M.A. 9 July, 1601, then in orders, rector of Colmore, Hants, 1609-17; father of Edward 1633, and of the next-named. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Roche, Robert, of Somerset, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 8 Nov., 1594, aged 18; B.A. 9 June, 1599, vicar of Hilton, Dorset, 1617, until his death 1629; father of Robert. See Ath. i. 682; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Clutterbuck, William (Cloterbooke), of co. Gloucester, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 8 Nov., 1594, aged 20; of Lincoln's Inn 1597. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Wilcock, William, of Kent, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 8 Nov., 1594, aged 16; B.A. from Magdalen Coll., 29 Jan., 1596-7.
Richerdsone, Augustine, of Kent, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 8 Nov., 1594, aged 24; B.A. 4 May, 1598.
Rishworth, Thomas, of co. Lincoln, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 10 Oct., 1594, aged 17.
Dickins, Robert, of co. Warwick, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 10 Oct., 1594, aged 17.
Sharpe, William, of co. Derby, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 12 April, 1594, aged 20.
Fisher, Thomas, of Wilts, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 8 Nov. 1594, aged 18.

Johnson, Robert, of Sussex, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 28 Nov., 1595, aged 18; demy Magdalen Coll. 1596-8, B.A. 8 July, 1598, M.A. 10 July, 1601, B.D. 8 Dec., 1619, chaplain to James I., vicar of Odiham 1604, of Newton Valence, Hants, 1614, of Selsey, Sussex, 1624, rector of St. Andrew 1625, and St. Olave in Chichester 1626, vicar of Willingdon, Sussex, 1626. See Ath. ii. 585; & Bloxam, iv. 240.

Greenfeild, Thomas (Grinfild), of Kent, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 27 Feb., 1595-6, aged 16; chorister Magdalen Coll. 1596, demy 1598-1605, B.A. 13 Dec., 1602, M.A. 3 July, 1605; perhaps vicar of Montacute, Somerset, 1609, vicar of Earl's Colne, Essex, 1610. See Foster's Index Eccl.; & Bloxam, i. 27.

Rumney, John, of Kent, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 27 Feb., 1595-6, aged 16, B.A. 13 Dec., 1599; a student of Middle Temple 1599 (as son and heir of John, of Maidstone, Kent, gent.); father of Norton. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Grimes, Walter, of co. Hereford, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 28 Nov., 1595, aged 17; B.A. 13 June, 1599, possibly of the Inner Temple 28 Feb., 1604-5, then attendant upon the prince. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Turbe(r)vile, George, of Dorset, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 28 Nov., 1595, aged 17; brother of Arundell.
Leigh, William, of co. Gloucester, arm. Magdalen Hall, matric. entry dated 28 Nov., 1595, aged 12.
Leeke, Thomas, of Northants, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 7 Nov., 1595, aged 16.
Hardinge, Henry, of Devon, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 28 Nov., 1595, aged 23.

Charlett, Francis, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 23 July, 1596, aged 16, B.A. 21 Oct., 1598; M.A. from All Souls' Coll. 8 July, 1602, then in orders, rector of Great Brickhill, Bucks, 1608, canon of Worcester 1627. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Hall, John, of Southants, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 30 July, 1596, aged 17; called "Haule" in Mat. Reg.; B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 9 July, 1600; M.A. from Corpus Christi Coll. 21 June, 1604, B.D. 8 July, 1613. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Simpson, Henry, of Northants, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 30 July, 1596, aged 17, B.A. 21 Feb., 1600-1, M.A. 6 July, 1609; vicar of Northleach with Eastington, co. Gloucester, 1620. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Add. MS. 15,671, f. 248.

Mar(r)iat, Richard, of Northants, cler. fil. Magdalen Hall, matric. 14 Jan., 1596-7, aged 19; B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 27 Nov., 1600; perhaps rector of Dummer, Hants, 1600-24; father of John 1627. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Brewer, Thomas, of Kent, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 30 July, 1596, aged 16; B.A. 2 May, 1604, student of Gray's Inn 1598, as son and heir of Robert, of Boxley, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Atkyns, Thomas, of co. Gloucester, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 30 July, 1596, aged 18; son and heir of Richard, bencher of Lincoln's Inn, admitted 1598. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
N(o)urse, Thomas, of co. Gloucester, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 3 April, 1596, aged 17; bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1608. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Davies, William, of Somerset, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 30 July, 1596, aged 14; B.A. 17 Feb., 1600-1; M.A. from Christ Church 27 June, 1607.
Christopher, Edward, of Middlesex, gent. Magdalen Hall., matric. 5 Nov., 1596, aged 18; demy Magdalen Coll., 1597-8. See Bloxam, iv. 240.
Cuff, William, of Somerset, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 19 Nov., 1596, aged 14; of Lincoln's Inn 1601. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Dabridgcourte, William, of Notts, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 30 July, 1596, aged 18; B.A. (sup. 15 Dec.) 1598, M.A. 26 June, 1601.
Ellis, Francis, of London, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 30 July, 1596, aged 15; one of these names vicar of Owthorne, Yorks, 1605.
Arris, Thomas (Aris), of Oxon, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 30 July, 1596, aged 17; B.A. from Brasenose Coll. 10 Feb., 1598-9.
Bennett, Roger, of co. Hereford, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 14 Oct., 1596, aged 13; B.A. 31 Oct., 1598, M.A. 10 July, 1601.
Hundlebie, Robert, of co. Lincoln, pleb. Magdalen Hall, matric. 14 Oct., 1596, aged 21; rector of Knebworth, Herts, 1605.
Grey, Thomas, of Southants, gent. Magdalen Hall, matric. 30 July, 1596, aged 18; B.A. 9 Feb., 1601-2.
